post_state_files.client.py:
from typing import Any, Dict, Optional

import httpx


class APIError(Exception):
    """Raised when the remote API returns a 4xx or 5xx status."""
    def __init__(self, status_code: int, body: Optional[str] = None):
        super().__init__(f'API error {status_code}: {body}')
        self.status_code = status_code
        self.body = body


class APIClient:
    """Async API client. Wraps httpx.AsyncClient."""

    def __init__(self, base_url: str, timeout: float = 5.0):
        self.base_url = base_url.rstrip('/')
        self.timeout = timeout

    async def fetch_user(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """GET /users/{user_id} → JSON dict. Raises APIError on 4xx/5xx,
        re-raises httpx.TransportError on network failure."""
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            r = await client.get(f'{self.base_url}/users/{user_id}')
            if r.status_code >= 400:
                raise APIError(r.status_code, r.text)
            return r.json()

    async def create_post(self, author_id: int, title: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """POST /posts. Body must be {author_id, title}. Returns the created post."""
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            r = await client.post(
                f'{self.base_url}/posts',
                json={'author_id': author_id, 'title': title},
            )
            if r.status_code >= 400:
                raise APIError(r.status_code, r.text)
            return r.json()

    async def ping(self) -> bool:
        """GET /healthz → True iff response is 200."""
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            try:
                r = await client.get(f'{self.base_url}/healthz')
                return r.status_code == 200
            except httpx.TransportError:
                return False
post_state_files.test_client.py:
import pytest
import httpx
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch

from client import APIClient, APIError


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_fetch_user_success():
    """Test fetch_user returns data on successful 200 response."""
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    
    mock_response = {
        "id": 1,
        "name": "John Doe",
        "email": "john@example.com"
    }
    
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "get", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_get:
        mock_get.return_value.status_code = 200
        mock_get.return_value.json.return_value = mock_response
        mock_get.return_value.text = ""
        
        result = await client.fetch_user(1)
        
        assert result == mock_response
        mock_get.assert_called_once()


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_fetch_user_4xx_error():
    """Test fetch_user raises APIError on 4xx response."""
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "get", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_get:
        mock_get.return_value.status_code = 404
        mock_get.return_value.text = "User not found"
        
        with pytest.raises(APIError) as exc_info:
            await client.fetch_user(999)
        
        assert exc_info.value.status_code == 404
        assert exc_info.value.body == "User not found"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_fetch_user_network_error():
    """Test fetch_user re-raises httpx.TransportError on network failure."""
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "get", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_get:
        mock_get.side_effect = httpx.TransportError("Connection failed")
        
        with pytest.raises(httpx.TransportError):
            await client.fetch_user(1)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_create_post_success():
    """Test create_post returns created post data on success."""
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    
    mock_response = {
        "id": 42,
        "author_id": 1,
        "title": "Test Post",
        "created_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    }
    
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "post", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_post:
        mock_post.return_value.status_code = 201
        mock_post.return_value.json.return_value = mock_response
        mock_post.return_value.text = ""
        
        result = await client.create_post(author_id=1, title="Test Post")
        
        assert result == mock_response
        mock_post.assert_called_once()
        call_args = mock_post.call_args
        assert call_args[1]["json"] == {"author_id": 1, "title": "Test Post"}


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_create_post_4xx_error():
    """Test create_post raises APIError on 4xx response."""
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "post", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_post:
        mock_post.return_value.status_code = 400
        mock_post.return_value.text = "Title is required"
        
        with pytest.raises(APIError) as exc_info:
            await client.create_post(author_id=1, title="")
        
        assert exc_info.value.status_code == 400
        assert exc_info.value.body == "Title is required"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_ping_success():
    """Test ping returns True on 200 response."""
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "get", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_get:
        mock_get.return_value.status_code = 200
        
        result = await client.ping()
        
        assert result is True


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_ping_not_200():
    """Test ping returns False when response is not 200."""
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "get", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_get:
        mock_get.return_value.status_code = 503
        
        result = await client.ping()
        
        assert result is False


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_ping_network_error():
    """Test ping returns False on network error."""
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "get", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_get:
        mock_get.side_effect = httpx.TransportError("Connection refused")
        
        result = await client.ping()
        
        assert result is False
